exercises.name: Rotating Side Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, keep your elbows underneath your shoulders at all times. 

2) Keep your abdominals and your glutes on to maintain a straight line throughout your body. 

3) Start in a plank position. 

4) Rotate as you open up reaching up to the ceiling. Keep your hips up and forward by squeezing your glutes. 

5) Return to the starting position. 

6) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Rotational Jump
exercises.description: Jump and rotate your torso mid-air before landing, engaging core and leg muscles.

exercises.name: Rotator Cuff Strengthening Exercises: Internal and External Rotation
exercises.description: Rotator Cuff Strengthening Exercises: Internal and External Rotation

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Contralateral Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: With one foot on the rower and dumbbell in opposite hand, step the other foot back. Lunge and push back to start.

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Place one foot on rower and hold dumbbells in both hands. Lunge back with opposite leg and drive up.

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Dumbbell Side Lunge
exercises.description: With one foot on the rower and dumbbells in hand, perform a side lunge. Keep chest tall and push back to center.

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Ipsilateral Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: One foot on the rower, dumbbell in the same side hand. Step back with opposite leg into a lunge, then return.

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Place one foot on the seat and step the other back into a reverse lunge. Drive through the front heel to return.

exercises.name: Rower Elevated Side Lunge
exercises.description: Place one foot on the rower and step the other leg out into a side lunge. Push through the grounded foot to return.

exercises.name: Rower High Plank Knee Tuck
exercises.description: In high plank, use the seat to slide knees toward your chest. Maintain control and avoid rounding the spine.

exercises.name: Rower High Plank Pike
exercises.description: In high plank, slide the seat forward while lifting your hips into a pike. Return slowly to plank.

exercises.name: Rower High Plank Rollout
exercises.description: In high plank with feet on the seat, roll the seat back by extending your arms forward. Keep core tight and hips stable.

exercises.name: Rower Low Plank Knee Tuck
exercises.description: In low plank with feet on the seat, draw knees toward your chest, keeping back flat. Extend legs to reset.

exercises.name: Rower Low Plank Pike
exercises.description: In low plank, keep legs straight and lift hips up into a pike position by sliding the seat forward. Lower with control.

exercises.name: Rower Low Plank Rollout
exercises.description: In low plank with forearms on the floor, feet on seat, roll the seat back by extending shoulders. Keep abs tight throughout.

exercises.name: Rowing Machine
exercises.description: Sit on the rower, strap in, and use legs, core, then arms to pull. Extend fully, then reverse: arms, core, legs.

exercises.name: Runner Stretch
exercises.alternateName: Warrior Pose Variation, hip and chest opener
exercises.description:
1) When performing this dynamic warm up, you’ll want to ensure that you will allow your hips to drop down to the ground when you’re in a stretch out position.

2) When you’re rotating your chest open, reach to the sky or ceiling rather than behind you.

exercises.name: Running in Place
exercises.alternateName: On the spot running, jog on spot
exercises.description:
1. Try to be light on your feet.

2. Maintain proper posture as you run on the spot.

3. Pull your knee up until your toes are pointing to the floor then back to the ground.

4. As one foot touches the ground, start pulling up the other knee until your toes are pointing to the floor then back to the ground.

5. While doing this, make sure your arms are also moving.

6. Do this as quickly as you can.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, it’s important to keep a short distance between your pelvis and your rib cage. 

2) That ensures that your abdominals are active don’t load your lower back by arching back. 

3) Once in position, twist your body to reach towards the ground with your selected weights or your hands. 

4) Alternate to the other side. 

5) Keep the weight close to your body and do your best not to lean back.

exercises.name: SA cable cross over lat pull down
